{"branch":"main","confidence":88,"prompt":"PLEASE IMPLEMENT THIS PLAN:
# NixOS GNUstep Linux Target Plan

## Summary
Use a phased full-app strategy on latest NixOS: ship a production-quality headless/core target first, then land the GNUstep desktop app on the same runtime and packaging model.

This plan is based on:
- existing Nix/GNUstep scaffolding in [flake.nix](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/flake.nix), [GNUmakefile](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/GNUmakefile), and [GNUmakefile.demo](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/GNUmakefile.demo)
- current Linux shims in [SSBNetworkCompat.h](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/Sources/SSBNetworkCompat.h), [SSBKeychain.m](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/Sources/SSBKeychain.m), and [AppDelegate.m](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/App/AppDelegate.m)
- the `gnustep-compat-audit` skill plus the repo’s `gnustep-porting` skill
- current GNUstep and Nix package docs:
  - [GNUstep API refs](https://developer.gnustep.org/APIRefs/index.html)
  - [NSURLSession docs](https://developer.gnustep.org/APIRefs/Base/BaseAPI/NSURLSession.html)
  - [NSToolbar docs](https://developer.gnustep.org/APIRefs/Gui/GuiAPI/NSToolbar.html)
  - [NSPasteboard docs](https://developer.gnustep.org/APIRefs/Gui/GuiAPI/NSPasteboard.html)
  - [GNUstep Make Tool reference](https://developer.gnustep.org/APIRefs/Make/ProjectTypes/Tool.html)
  - [NixOS `gnustep-base`](https://mynixos.com/nixpkgs/package/gnustep-base)
  - [NixOS `gnustep-gui`](https://mynixos.com/nixpkgs/package/gnustep-gui)
  - [NixOS `gnustep-back`](https://mynixos.com/nixpkgs/package/gnustep-back)
  - [NixOS `swift-corelibs-libdispatch`](https://mynixos.com/nixpkgs/package/swift-corelibs-libdispatch)

## Key Changes
### 1. Define the Linux target explicitly
- Linux target means two deliverables:
  - `scuttle-cli`: fully functional on NixOS with identity, local DB/blob store, invite parsing, room connectivity, feed publish/read, and git helper support where protocol features already exist.
  - `Scuttle.app` for GNUstep: launches under `gnustep-gui`/`gnustep-back`, renders the main UI, and supports the same local account/room workflow as macOS except for explicitly downgraded Apple-only integrations.
- First-class runtime is NixOS `nixos-unstable` from the existing flake, on `x86_64-linux`; `aarch64-linux` is kept build-clean but not a release gate for v1.
- GUI validation target is X11/XWayland via `gnustep-back`; Wayland-native behavior is not a v1 requirement.

### 2. Stop treating Apple APIs as the cross-platform interface
- Replace direct Apple framework dependence with repo-owned platform seams:
  - `SSBSecretStore` protocol replacing hard dependency on `Security.framework` keychain calls in [SSBKeychain.m](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/Sources/SSBKeychain.m).
  - `SSBTransport` / `SSBListener` / framer-facing transport interfaces replacing repo-wide use of `nw_*` as the internal contract.
  - `SRPlatformNotifications` for user-visible notifications.
  - `SRPlatformUIImports` or equivalent shared import header so app code stops importing `Cocoa/Cocoa.h` directly.
- Keep Apple implementations as adapters:
  - macOS transport adapter may still use `Network.framework`.
  - macOS secret store may still use Security.framework.
  - macOS notifications may still use UserNotifications.
- Linux implementations:
  - secret store: encrypted file store in XDG config/state location with `0600` permissions; no fake keychain API.
  - transport: libdispatch + BSD sockets/TLS/OpenSSL; no emulated `Network.framework` public surface.
  - notifications: best-effort `libnotify` adapter if available, otherwise no-op with in-app banners/logging.
- Decision: do not expand the current `nw_*` shim into a full fake `Network.framework`. The existing shim is already declared “intended for linkage only” in [SSBNetworkCompat.h:13](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/Sources/SSBNetworkCompat.h:13), and a repo-owned transport layer is safer than chasing Apple semantics on Linux.

### 3. Use GNUstep where it is real, remove local shims where it is not needed
- Adopt GNUstep Foundation/AppKit directly for APIs that exist:
  - `Foundation/Foundation.h`
  - `AppKit/AppKit.h`
  - GNUstep `NSURLSession` instead of the local compatibility declaration in [SSBURLSessionCompat.h](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/Sources/SSBURLSessionCompat.h:1)
  - GNUstep `NSToolbar`, `NSPasteboard`, menus, windows, split views
- Remove or shrink shims that only duplicate GNUstep APIs:
  - eliminate `SSBURLSessionCompat`/`SSBURLSessionShim` if GNUstep behavior is sufficient for current invite/HTTP use
  - keep `SSBLogCompat` and `SSBCommonCryptoCompat` as thin compatibility layers
- Convert app imports:
  - replace `#import <Cocoa/Cocoa.h>` in app/UI headers with shared platform header mapping to `AppKit` + `Foundation` on Linux
  - isolate GNUstep/AppKit gaps in a small number of adapter files, not scattered preprocessor guards

### 4. Make Nix the source of truth for build, test, and packaging
- Expand the existing flake into explicit outputs:
  - `packages.x86_64-linux.scuttle-cli`
  - `packages.x86_64-linux.scuttle-gui`
  - `apps` entries for both
  - `checks` for headless and GUI smoke tests
- Keep GNUmakefile-based builds inside Nix for Objective-C targets; do not introduce a second unrelated build system.
- Add a dedicated GNUstep app makefile for the real GUI target, not just the demo.
- Shell/build environment must pin:
  - `gnustep-make`
  - `gnustep-base`
  - `gnustep-gui`
  - `gnustep-back`
  - `gnustep-libobjc`
  - `swift-corelibs-libdispatch`
  - `openssl`
  - `sqlite`
  - X11/font/cairo dependencies
- CI and local docs use `nix develop`, `nix build`, and `nix run` only. No apt-style setup steps in the canonical workflow.

### 5. Deliver in four milestones
- Milestone A: Core Linux runtime
  - build `scuttle-cli` cleanly under NixOS
  - replace keychain dependency
  - remove URL session shim if GNUstep session works
  - make XDG paths authoritative
- Milestone B: Transport hardening
  - introduce repo-owned transport interface
  - port room/tunnel/framer stack off direct `nw_*` usage
  - validate connect/send/receive/listen paths against local room/tunnel smoke tests
- Milestone C: GNUstep desktop bring-up
  - real app target with main window, split view, toolbar, menus, compose/feed/profile/repo views
  - disable or replace Apple-only status bar and UserNotifications behavior
  - make startup and room-manager lifecycle stable on GNUstep
- Milestone D: Packaging and release readiness
  - `nix run` launches app
  - `nix build` emits both CLI and GUI artifacts
  - docs describe NixOS install/run/debug path
  - acceptance checklist passes on fresh NixOS machine/VM

## Public Interfaces / Types
- Add `SSBSecretStore` protocol and two implementations:
  - `SSBAppleKeychainSecretStore`
  - `SSBFileSecretStore`
- Add `SSBTransportBackend` interfaces for connection/listener/framer data flow, with:
  - Apple backend
  - Linux backend
- Add `SRPlatformNotifications` interface with:
  - Apple notifications adapter
  - Linux `libnotify` or no-op adapter
- Add one shared UI import/platform header for AppKit/Foundation selection.
- Deprecate direct use of:
  - `SSBKeychain` as the persistence abstraction
  - `nw_*` types as repo-internal business logic types
  - `SSBURLSessionCompat` as a Linux substitute for Foundation networking

## Test Plan
- Build checks
  - `nix build .#scuttle-cli`
  - `nix build .#scuttle-gui`
  - `nix flake check`
- Core runtime tests
  - generate/load identity and metafeed on Linux file-backed secret store
  - publish/read/follow/timeline against local SQLite/blob stores
  - room invite parsing and HTTP fetch using GNUstep `NSURLSession`
- Transport tests
  - local listener creation
  - client connect/send/receive over libdispatch+sockets
  - tunnel smoke test
  - framer stack round-trip for muxrpc/security framing
- GUI tests
  - app launches under Xvfb
  - main window appears
  - toolbar renders and actions wire up
  - pasteboard copy path works
  - room list/menu updates
  - unsupported Apple-only features degrade without crash
- Acceptance scenarios
  - fresh NixOS shell: `nix develop` then build both targets successfully
  - fresh NixOS run: initialize identity, connect to a room, publish a message from CLI
  - fresh NixOS GUI: launch app, open main window, browse local data, connect to configured room

## Assumptions and Defaults
- Primary Linux target is latest NixOS on `nixos-unstable`, matching the existing flake input.
- v1 GUI backend is X11/XWayland through `gnustep-back`; Wayland-native support is deferred.
- GNUstep APIs documented today are treated as preferred over local shims when behavior is sufficient.
- UserNotifications and Security.framework are not portability targets; they are replaced by Linux-native adapters.
- The current CLI target in [GNUmakefile](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/GNUmakefile) is treated as a starting point, not a finished contract.
- The existing `Network.framework` shim is transitional and should be retired behind a repo-owned transport abstraction rather than expanded."}

{"branch":"main","confidence":94,"prompt":"PLEASE IMPLEMENT THIS PLAN:
# Programmatic AppKit UI Skill Companion

## Summary
Create a new companion skill, `objc-appkit-programmatic-ui`, under `/Users/jack/.codex/skills`, optimized for both building and reviewing Objective-C AppKit UIs that are created entirely in code, with no xib/storyboard path by default. Keep the existing broad skill at [SKILL.md](/Users/jack/.codex/skills/objc-appkit-cocoa/SKILL.md) intact, but add a short cross-link so prompts that explicitly say “programmatic AppKit”, “no nib/xib/storyboard”, or “review this code-built UI” route to the new companion.

## Public Interface
- New skill metadata:
  - `name: objc-appkit-programmatic-ui`
  - Description should explicitly mention Objective-C, AppKit, programmatic UI only, `NSWindowController`/`NSViewController`, Auto Layout, `NSStackView`, `NSSplitViewController`, toolbars/menus, accessibility, and review/audit workflows.
- New `agents/openai.yaml` for user-facing discovery with a default prompt that explicitly invokes `$objc-appkit-programmatic-ui`.
- Existing `objc-appkit-cocoa` keeps its current name and broad mixed-UI role; only add a delegation note to the new companion for programmatic-only work.
- Do not change `dist/` in v1.

## Implementation Changes
- Add `SKILL.md` that encodes a strict programmatic workflow:
  - Detect app entry and ownership chain first: `NSApplicationDelegate` -> `NSWindowController` -> root `NSViewController` -> child controllers/views.
  - Default to code-built windows/views and explicitly avoid suggesting nibs/storyboards unless the user asks for them.
  - Prefer anchor-based Auto Layout, `translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = NO`, `NSStackView` for linear layouts, `NSGridView` for dense forms, and `NSSplitViewController` for sidebar/detail shells.
  - Include a review rubric covering controller ownership, lifecycle correctness, constraint hygiene, CHCR priorities, responder chain, toolbar/menu validation, accessibility, localization, and main-thread UI rules.
  - Include a debugging loop for ambiguous/unsatisfiable layout, broken intrinsic sizing, scroll-view containment, and oversized nested stack-view trees.
- Add focused references:
  - `references/architecture.md` for no-nib app bootstrap, window/controller boundaries, and programmatic containment.
  - `references/layout-and-debugging.md` for anchors, CHCR, intrinsic size, ambiguous/unsatisfiable layouts, and debugging heuristics.
  - `references/containers-and-controls.md` for split views, toolbars, search, sidebars, tables/outline views, and form composition.
  - `references/review-rubric.md` for evaluation criteria and common failure patterns.
  - `references/accessibility-and-testing.md` for AppKit accessibility expectations and practical XCTest/UI-test guidance.
  - `references/source-map.md` as a curated link map separating Apple-official sources from secondary conceptual material.
- Add reusable Objective-C assets:
  - `assets/minimal-programmatic-app/` with `main.m`, `AppDelegate`, `MainWindowController`, and root controller wired entirely in code.
  - `assets/sidebar-split-view/` with `NSSplitViewController` sidebar/detail scaffolding and toolbar-ready window setup.
  - `assets/form-and-inspector/` with `NSStackView`/`NSGridView` composition patterns for settings, inspector, or metadata panels.
  - Templates should be ARC, macOS 13+, concise, and comment only where the ownership/layout pattern is non-obvious.

## Test Plan
- Validate triggering with representative prompts:
  - “Build a programmatic AppKit prefs window in Objective-C.”
  - “Review this no-xib AppKit layout code.”
  - “Add a sidebar/detail shell with toolbar search.”
- Acceptance criteria:
  - The skill never recommends xib/storyboard unless explicitly requested.
  - Output consistently chooses correct ownership and lifecycle seams for code-built windows.
  - Review output flags layout/accessibility/HIG issues instead of only restating structure.
  - Assets are internally consistent and suitable as copyable starting points.
- Smoke-check the templates after implementation with a minimal syntax/build pass in a scratch harness; fix any compile-time API mismatches before considering the skill complete.

## Assumptions
- Target platform is macOS 13+ Ventura, matching the project’s minimum.
- Apple documentation is authoritative when it conflicts with secondary sources.
- Secondary sources should be used only for conceptual framing, not as the source of record for API behavior.
- The new skill stays narrow and high-signal; detailed material belongs in `references/`, not in a bloated `SKILL.md`.

## Research Basis
- Apple: [Designing for macOS](https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/designing-for-macos)
- Apple: [Accessibility](https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/accessibility)
- Apple: [The Core App Design](https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/MOSXAppProgrammingGuide/CoreAppDesign/CoreAppDesign.html)
- Apple: [View Programming Guide for Cocoa](https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/Cocoa/Conceptual/CocoaViewsGuide/Introduction/Introduction.html)
- Apple: [Auto Layout Guide: Programmatically Creating Constraints](https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/UserExperience/Conceptual/AutolayoutPG/ProgrammaticallyCreatingConstraints.html)
- Apple: [Auto Layout Guide: Unsatisfiable Layouts](https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/UserExperience/Conceptual/AutolayoutPG/ConflictingLayouts.html)
- Apple: [Auto Layout Guide: Views with Intrinsic Content Size](https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/UserExperience/Conceptual/AutolayoutPG/ViewswithIntrinsicContentSize.html)
- Apple: [Introduction to Accessibility Programming Guidelines for Cocoa](https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/Cocoa/Conceptual/Accessibility/cocoaAXIntro/cocoaAXintro.html)
- Apple: [Organize Your User Interface with a Stack View](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/AppKit/organize-your-user-interface-with-a-stack-view)
- Apple: [Navigating Hierarchical Data Using Outline and Split Views](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/appkit/navigating-hierarchical-data-using-outline-and-split-views)
- Apple: [Integrating a Toolbar and Touch Bar into Your App](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/appkit/integrating-a-toolbar-and-touch-bar-into-your-app)
- Apple: [Adopt the new look of macOS](https://developer.apple.com/br/videos/play/wwdc2020/10104/)
- Secondary: [NSHipster: UIStackView](https://nshipster.com/uistackview/) as stack-layout intuition only, not normative AppKit guidance."}

nodes.metadata_json:
{"branch":"main","confidence":92,"prompt":"# Implement the Required `Network.framework` Subset, Especially Framers

## Summary
Do not try to reimplement all of `Network.framework`. Implement the exact subset this repo uses, and make the framer runtime the center of the design.

The right shape is:
- keep Apple builds on real `Network.framework`
- keep `SSBNetworkCompat.h` as the Linux-facing API surface
- replace the current shim internals with a real per-connection runtime for:
  - endpoints
  - parameters/protocol stack
  - connections
  - listeners
  - content contexts / metadata
  - `nw_framer` definitions, options, per-connection instances, and message delivery

The implementation target is “behavior compatible enough for `SSBSecurityFramer` and `SSBMuxRPCFramer`”, not a general-purpose clone of Apple networking.

## Key Changes

### 1. Define the supported API subset explicitly
Implement only these functions/types from [SSBNetworkCompat.h](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/Sources/SSBNetworkCompat.h):
- Endpoints:
  - `nw_endpoint_create_host`
- Parameters / protocol stack:
  - `nw_parameters_create_secure_tcp`
  - `nw_parameters_copy_default_protocol_stack`
  - `nw_parameters_set_local_endpoint`
  - `nw_protocol_stack_prepend_application_protocol`
  - `nw_tcp_options_set_no_delay`
- Connections:
  - `nw_connection_create`
  - `nw_connection_set_queue`
  - `nw_connection_set_state_changed_handler`
  - `nw_connection_start`
  - `nw_connection_receive`
  - `nw_connection_receive_message`
  - `nw_connection_send`
  - `nw_connection_cancel`
- Listeners:
  - `nw_listener_create`
  - `nw_listener_set_queue`
  - `nw_listener_set_state_changed_handler`
  - `nw_listener_set_new_connection_handler`
  - `nw_listener_start`
  - `nw_listener_cancel`
  - `nw_listener_get_port`
- Framer/runtime:
  - `nw_framer_create_definition`
  - `nw_framer_create_options`
  - `nw_framer_copy_options`
  - `nw_framer_options_set_object_value`
  - `nw_framer_options_copy_object_value`
  - `nw_framer_set_input_handler`
  - `nw_framer_set_output_handler`
  - `nw_framer_mark_ready`
  - `nw_framer_mark_failed_with_error`
  - `nw_framer_parse_input`
  - `nw_framer_parse_output`
  - `nw_framer_write_output_data`
  - `nw_framer_deliver_input`
  - `nw_framer_message_create`
  - `nw_framer_message_set_object_value`
  - `nw_framer_message_copy_object_value`
  - `nw_content_context_copy_protocol_metadata`

Anything outside that subset stays unsupported and unimplemented.

### 2. Replace the current shim with real internal runtime objects
Keep the public compat typedefs as opaque `id`, but back them with concrete internal classes in [SSBNetworkShim.m](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/Sources/SSBNetworkShim.m):
- `SSBNWEndpoint`
- `SSBNWParameters`
- `SSBNWProtocolStack`
- `SSBNWConnection`
- `SSBNWListener`
- `SSBNWFramerDefinition`
- `SSBNWFramerOptions`
- `SSBNWFramerInstance`
- `SSBNWFramerMessage`
- `SSBNWContentContext`

Important design choice:
- framer definitions are shared templates
- framer options are per stack entry
- framer instances are per connection
- message/context objects are per delivered message

Do not reuse one `SSBFramerContext` across all connections. That is the main correctness boundary.

### 3. Implement the framer runtime as a pipeline, not as ad hoc callbacks
The framer layer needs two explicit pipelines per connection:

Inbound:
- socket bytes enter raw read buffer
- bytes are offered to the lowest framer first
- each framer consumes from its own inbound source buffer via `nw_framer_parse_input`
- `nw_framer_deliver_input` passes bytes and message metadata upward
- once the top framer delivers a message, it is queued for `nw_connection_receive` / `receive_message`

Outbound:
- `nw_connection_send` creates an outbound message/context
- top framer `output_handler` runs first
- `nw_framer_parse_output` exposes outbound payload bytes to that framer
- `nw_framer_write_output_data` passes transformed bytes down to the next framer
- bottom framer writes final bytes to the socket

Required semantics:
- `nw_framer_create_definition` stores identifier and start handler
- `nw_framer_create_options` attaches options to a definition, not a shared instance
- `start_handler` runs once per per-connection framer instance
- `nw_framer_mark_ready` marks only that framer instance ready
- `nw_framer_mark_failed_with_error` fails the owning connection
- `nw_framer_parse_input` must never call `recv` directly behind the framer’s back; it must read from the instance’s staged inbound buffer
- `nw_framer_parse_output` must expose the actual outbound message bytes, not recursively call the output handler
- `nw_framer_deliver_input` must queue delivered messages even if no receive callback is currently waiting
- `nw_content_context_copy_protocol_metadata` must return the metadata attached to the delivered message/context

### 4. Implement connection/listener behavior around the framer runtime
For `SSBNWConnection`:
- nonblocking socket
- dispatch read/write sources
- explicit state transitions:
  - `invalid -> preparing -> waiting -> ready`
  - `failed`
  - `cancelled`
- connect completion via write readiness + `SO_ERROR`
- receive API behavior:
  - if queued delivered message/data exists, fulfill immediately
  - else store pending receive request
- send API behavior:
  - preserve provided context/metadata through the framer chain
  - support both raw and framed sends
- adopted accepted connections must build the same framer stack as outbound connections when higher layers request it

For `SSBNWListener`:
- honor `nw_parameters_set_local_endpoint`
- bind requested host/port when provided
- keep ephemeral-port behavior for `port=0`
- accepted sockets must inherit queueing/state machinery

### 5. Decide TLS scope explicitly
Do not implement general TLS in this layer now.
- current repo traffic of interest is:
  - plain TCP
  - SHS framer
  - MuxRPC framer
- `nw_parameters_create_secure_tcp` should preserve the API shape, but Linux implementation can ignore TLS configuration for now
- if later room invite or HTTPS paths need TLS at this transport boundary, add it as a separate layer with clear requirements

### 6. Make the runtime satisfy the actual framers in this repo
`SSBSecurityFramer` requires:
- options lookup
- staged handshake parsing
- repeated parse/input cycles without recursion bugs
- `mark_ready`
- `mark_failed_with_error`
- output buffering before handshake completion
- delivery of decrypted payload upward as messages

`SSBMuxRPCFramer` requires:
- header/body staged parsing
- zero-length body delivery
- metadata on delivered messages:
  - `"Flags"`
  - `"RequestNumber"`
- output path that sees the actual serialized message bytes

The Linux runtime should be considered correct when these two framers run unchanged.

## Public Interfaces / Types
Public API should stay at the existing compat layer in [SSBNetworkCompat.h](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/Sources/SSBNetworkCompat.h) and the transport seam in [SSBTransport.h](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/Sources/SSBTransport.h).

Internal additions should be private to the shim implementation:
- definition/options/instance/message/context runtime classes
- per-connection inbound delivered-message queue
- per-connection pending receive queue
- per-connection framer stack instantiated from parameter protocol options

No new public repo API is required unless you want a debug hook. If added, keep it internal-only.

## Test Plan

### Framer runtime unit tests
Add focused tests that do real behavior, not “does not crash”:
- `SSBMuxRPCFramer`
  - header parse
  - body parse
  - zero-length body
  - metadata delivery
  - output serialization path
- `SSBSecurityFramer`
  - client/server handshake progresses to ready
  - encrypted payload round-trip
  - goodbye / failure path

### Shim runtime tests
Add Linux-shim-specific tests for:
- per-connection framer instance isolation
- queued delivery before receive callback registration
- receive callback waiting before delivery
- listener accept path
- adopted connection path
- local endpoint bind semantics
- state transitions on connect success/failure/cancel

### Integration tests
Run through the transport seam:
- `SSBTransportTests` loopback path
- room client connect/send/receive over shim backend
- tunnel connection loopback bridge
- mux metadata propagation from framer to [SSBTransport.m](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/Sources/SSBTransport.m)

### Acceptance scenarios
The implementation is complete when:
- macOS still uses native `Network.framework` unchanged
- Linux path no longer depends on “intended for linkage only” behavior
- `SSBSecurityFramer` and `SSBMuxRPCFramer` both operate unchanged on Linux
- room/tunnel happy path works through the compat layer
- GNUstep CLI and GUI builds no longer rely on a fake/no-op framer path

## Assumptions and Defaults
- We are implementing the subset the repo uses, not a full `Network.framework` clone.
- TLS is deferred unless a concrete current call path requires it.
- The framer runtime is the primary deliverable; sockets/listeners are secondary plumbing.
- The transport seam remains the architectural boundary for higher-level code.
- Existing Apple behavior is the reference semantics where the repo depends on them."}

nodes.metadata_json:
{"branch":"main","confidence":90,"prompt":"PLEASE IMPLEMENT THIS PLAN:
# Fix GUI Peer Sync State

## Summary
The GUI peer-sync bug is primarily a state-propagation problem, not a peer-discovery problem. Tunnel EBT updates are recorded in per-peer session state, but the progress/UI path reads only the room-level `remoteClock`, so peers can stay stuck at early statuses like `Connecting...` or `Handshaking...` even while replication is active. The fix should make sync state authoritative per room/client, then have the peer list, profile view, and sidebar all read that same room-scoped state.

## Key Changes
- In [SSBRoomClient.m](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/Sources/SSBRoomClient.m), replace the current `updateSyncProgressForAuthor:` flow with a room-local, source-aware lookup:
  - Add a helper that resolves the remote sequence for an author from either `remoteClock` or the matching `peerEBTState[peerID][@"clock"]`.
  - Call that helper from both remote-clock updates and replicated-message updates, passing the source peer/session so tunnel replication produces real `Receiving`/`Sending`/`Ready` transitions.
  - Keep `peerSyncProgress` and `peerSyncStates` as the authoritative per-client snapshot, and mutate them on `clientQueue`.
  - Stop posting `SRRoomSyncStatusChangedNotification` directly from the client; the client should notify only its delegate so there is a single notification source.

- In [SRRoomManager.m](/Users/jack/Software/Scuttle/App/Logic/SRRoomManager.m), make the manager the room-scoped sync cache and notification bridge:
  - Cache peer sync status/progress by room host.
  - Cache the latest room-level summary status/progress per host for the sidebar.
  - Add public read APIs for `peerSyncStatesForHost:`, `peerSyncProgressForHost:`, and selected-room summary status/progress.
  - Emit `SRRoomSyncStatusChangedNotification` only from the manager, always including `host`, `status`, `progress`, and optional `author`.
  - Add explicit notification userInfo key constants in `SRNotificationNames.h` so the UI stops relying on string literals.

- In the GUI layer, make every consumer host-filtered instead of global:
  - `SRMainSplitViewController` sets the selected room host on the peer list and refreshes it from manager caches on room selection and endpoint changes.
  - `SRPeerListViewController` adds a `roomHost`/configuration entry point, reloads from manager snapshots for that host, ignores sync notifications for other hosts, and clears stale state when the selected room changes.
  - `SRProfileViewController` filters sync notifications to its own client/host before updating the header progress.
  - `SRSidebarViewController` tracks the selected room and shows sync only for that room, not “last notification wins” across all rooms.

## Public Interfaces / Types
- Add room-scoped sync accessors on `SRRoomManager`:
  - `peerSyncStatesForHost:`
  - `peerSyncProgressForHost:`
  - `syncStatusForHost:`
  - `syncProgressForHost:`
- Add notification keys in `SRNotificationNames.h` for:
  - sync host
  - sync status
  - sync progress
  - sync author
- Add a room-selection/configuration property or method on `SRPeerListViewController` so it can bind to one room at a time.

## Test Plan
- Extend `SSBRoomProtocolBugfixTests` or add a focused room-client test class to verify that:
  - a remote clock stored under `peerEBTState` updates `peerSyncStates`/`peerSyncProgress`
  - tunneled message replication transitions a peer from `Handshaking...` to `Receiving...` or `Ready`
- Add manager tests to verify:
  - sync state is cached per host
  - notification payloads always include host
  - duplicate client+manager sync notifications no longer occur
- Add controller logic tests for:
  - peer list ignores sync notifications from non-selected rooms
  - switching rooms clears stale peer statuses and reloads the new room snapshot
  - profile view ignores sync events from other clients/rooms
  - sidebar shows only the selected room’s latest status
- Run `SSBNetworkTests`, with targeted coverage for the new room-client and controller tests.

## Acceptance Scenarios
- A peer discovered in the selected room progresses visibly from `Connecting...` to `Handshaking...` to `Receiving...`/`Ready` during tunnel replication.
- Switching from room A to room B never shows A’s peer statuses in B’s peer list, profile header, or sidebar.
- Sidebar sync text reflects only the selected room.
- Peer discovery behavior for `room.attendants` and legacy `tunnel.endpoints` remains unchanged.

## Assumptions
- Keep the current UI copy and overall layout; this is a correctness/state-model fix, not a UX redesign.
- Scope is the GUI-facing sync path plus the room-client state needed to make that path correct; deeper transport hardening is out of scope unless the new tests show the UI still lacks valid clock updates.
- The selected-room-only sidebar behavior is the intended default."}
